title: Data Structures and Algorithms
summary: A comprehensive shared DSA curriculum with conceptual mastery preserved across C, C++, Java and Python and implementation mastery assessed separately in the learner-selected language.

audience.level: language-fluent beginner to intermediate computer-science learner
assumed_knowledge: Functions, arrays or sequences, loops, recursion basics, testing and debugging in one supported language
audience.target_capability: Select, implement, analyze, test and explain core data structures and algorithmic strategies in C, C++, Java or Python and transfer conceptual mastery when switching languages.

includes: Algorithm specification, correctness and asymptotic analysis
Linked lists, stacks, queues, deques and hashing
Trees, BSTs, tries, heaps and priority queues
Graph representation, traversal, topological order, shortest paths and spanning trees
Common sequence and range problem patterns
Cross-language implementation, testing and trade-off comparison
non_goals: Competitive-programming trick catalogs without underlying reasoning

authoritative_sources.title: GNU C Language Introduction and Reference Manual

runtime.language: Learner-selected C, C++, Java or Python
runtime.standard: C23; C++20 baseline; Java SE 21 LTS; Python 3.14
toolchain: Pinned compiler or interpreter from the selected language manifest
Memory sanitizer for C and C++
runtime.execution_environment: Isolated Linux runners with equivalent black-box tests and language-appropriate structural and safety checks
notes: Conceptual mastery is stored independently from language implementation mastery.
A language switch preserves concept evidence but retests required syntax, library, ownership and test-harness skills.
Failed switch diagnostics reopen the failed implementation prerequisite subgraph, not conceptual DSA from lesson one.

modules.title: Readiness, algorithms and analysis
modules.description: Establish implementation readiness and a precise model for correctness and cost.

skills.title: Selected-language readiness
skills.description: Demonstrate functions, sequences, recursion, testing and debugging in the selected language before DSA implementation.
outcomes: Pass an implementation diagnostic or receive a targeted prerequisite remediation map.

skills.title: Algorithm specification and invariants
skills.description: State inputs, outputs, preconditions, postconditions and a useful loop or structure invariant.
outcomes: Specify and trace an algorithm using a contract and invariant.

skills.title: Correctness and counterexamples
skills.description: Use cases, invariants and counterexamples to distinguish plausible code from a correct algorithm.
outcomes: Find a counterexample to an incorrect algorithm and explain a corrected invariant.

skills.title: Time, space and asymptotic growth
skills.description: Count dominant operations and use O, Omega and Theta to compare growth independent of one machine.
outcomes: Derive and compare time and auxiliary-space bounds for iterative and nested algorithms.

modules.title: Arrays, strings and matrices
modules.description: Master contiguous indexed data and two-dimensional traversal patterns.

skills.title: Array representation and operations
skills.description: Analyze indexed access, traversal, insertion, deletion, resizing and copying costs.
outcomes: Implement and test core array operations and explain their complexity.

skills.title: Two-dimensional arrays and matrices
skills.description: Traverse rectangular and ragged grids by row, column, neighborhood, diagonal and boundary.
outcomes: Implement and test a matrix or grid transformation with correct dimensions and bounds.

skills.title: String traversal and matching basics
skills.description: Process character sequences and implement naive substring, palindrome and frequency algorithms with encoding caveats labeled.
outcomes: Implement and analyze a text-processing algorithm in the selected language.

skills.title: Prefix and difference representations
skills.description: Precompute cumulative information or range deltas to answer or apply repeated interval operations efficiently.
outcomes: Recognize, implement and analyze one-dimensional and two-dimensional prefix-sum problems.

modules.title: Linked lists
modules.description: Reason about node links, ownership, edge cases and operation costs.

skills.title: Singly linked representation
skills.description: Model head, node value, next link and empty or singleton states.
outcomes: Draw, traverse and implement a singly linked list representation in the selected language.

skills.title: Insertion and deletion
skills.description: Update links correctly at head, middle and tail while preserving ownership and size invariants.
outcomes: Implement, debug and test list insertion and deletion across empty, singleton and multi-node cases.

skills.title: Doubly and circular lists
skills.description: Maintain bidirectional or cyclic invariants and compare added flexibility with added update risk.
outcomes: Implement and test a doubly linked operation and explain circular termination conditions.

skills.title: Two-pointer list patterns
skills.description: Use slow-fast and predecessor-current pointers for reversal, midpoint and cycle detection.
outcomes: Implement, prove informally and test reversal and cycle detection.

modules.title: Stacks, queues and deques
modules.description: Implement and apply restricted-access linear abstract data types.

skills.title: Stack ADT
skills.description: Specify and implement push, pop, peek and empty behavior using an appropriate backing store.
outcomes: Implement and test a stack and explain each operation's complexity.

skills.title: Queue and circular buffer
skills.description: Specify FIFO behavior and implement a bounded or dynamically backed queue without shifting every item.
outcomes: Implement and test enqueue, dequeue and wraparound behavior.

skills.title: Deque ADT
skills.description: Support insertion and removal at both ends and identify deque-suitable problems.
outcomes: Use or implement a deque and justify it over a stack, queue or list.

skills.title: Stack and queue applications
skills.description: Apply stacks to nesting or evaluation and queues to buffering or breadth-first work.
outcomes: Implement a delimiter checker and one queue-driven simulation with tests.

modules.title: Hash tables, sets and maps
modules.description: Use and understand hash-based lookup, collision handling and key contracts.

skills.title: Hash and equality contract
skills.description: Map equal keys to equal hashes and keep keys stable while stored.
outcomes: Design and test a key equality and hashing policy in the selected language.

skills.title: Collision resolution
skills.description: Explain chaining and open addressing with load factor, probing and clustering trade-offs.
outcomes: Trace insert, find and delete through collisions in a teaching hash table.

skills.title: Resizing and expected complexity
skills.description: Relate capacity and load factor to amortized expected operations and adversarial caveats.
outcomes: Implement or simulate rehashing and derive its amortized effect.

skills.title: Frequency and lookup patterns
skills.description: Use sets and maps for membership, counting, indexing and complements without unnecessary nested scans.
outcomes: Recognize and solve lookup and frequency problems with justified expected complexity.

modules.title: Searching and sorting
modules.description: Implement, compare and select fundamental search and ordering algorithms.

skills.title: Linear and binary search
skills.description: Search unsorted or sorted ranges with correct invariants and boundary updates.
outcomes: Implement, trace and test linear and binary search including empty and duplicate cases.

skills.title: Selection and insertion sort
skills.description: Use elementary sorts to practice invariants, stability and quadratic growth.
outcomes: Implement, trace and compare selection and insertion sort on representative inputs.

skills.title: Merge sort and quicksort
skills.description: Apply divide-and-conquer to stable merging and partition-based sorting with worst-case awareness.
outcomes: Implement and test merge sort and partition logic and compare their space and case behavior.

skills.title: Sorting properties and selection
skills.description: Compare stability, adaptiveness, in-place behavior, bounds and library guarantees.
outcomes: Select a sorting strategy for a real requirement and justify algorithm versus library use.

modules.title: Recursion and divide-and-conquer
modules.description: Design recursive solutions, derive recurrences and control resource use.

skills.title: Recursive problem design
skills.description: Identify a base case, smaller subproblem, combination step and progress measure.
outcomes: Design and implement a terminating recursive algorithm from a recursive structure.

skills.title: Call stack and resource cost
skills.description: Trace frames and relate recursion depth to auxiliary space and failure risk.
outcomes: Visualize calls and convert a deeply linear recursion to iteration when appropriate.

skills.title: Recurrence reasoning
skills.description: Express recursive work as a recurrence and estimate common growth patterns.
outcomes: Derive and solve or bound simple decrease-by-one and divide-by-factor recurrences.

skills.title: Divide-and-conquer strategy
skills.description: Divide independent subproblems, solve recursively and combine with controlled duplication.
outcomes: Design and analyze a divide-and-conquer solution beyond a memorized sorting example.

modules.title: Trees, search trees and tries
modules.description: Represent hierarchical data and maintain ordered-tree invariants.

skills.title: Tree terminology and representation
skills.description: Use root, parent, child, leaf, depth, height and subtree accurately for binary and n-ary trees.
outcomes: Represent and annotate a tree and compute node depths and tree height.

skills.title: Depth and breadth traversals
skills.description: Implement preorder, inorder, postorder and level-order traversals iteratively or recursively.
outcomes: Choose, implement and test a traversal for a stated hierarchical task.

skills.title: Binary search tree operations
skills.description: Maintain ordering through search, insertion and deletion and relate shape to cost.
outcomes: Implement and test BST operations including zero-, one- and two-child deletion.

skills.title: Balanced trees and tries
skills.description: Explain balance guarantees and implement trie insert, lookup and prefix traversal.
outcomes: Compare balanced BST and trie suitability and implement a tested trie.

modules.title: Heaps and priority queues
modules.description: Maintain partial order for repeated minimum or maximum selection.

skills.title: Heap property and array layout
skills.description: Map complete binary tree relationships to array indexes and distinguish heap from sorted order.
outcomes: Trace parent-child indexes and validate a min- or max-heap.

skills.title: Heapify, insert and extract
skills.description: Restore heap order upward or downward after structural changes.
outcomes: Implement and test heap construction, insertion and root extraction.

skills.title: Priority queue applications
skills.description: Use prioritized removal for scheduling, top-k selection and graph frontiers with stable tie policy when required.
outcomes: Solve and analyze a priority-driven problem with the selected language's library or implementation.

skills.title: Heapsort and selection
skills.description: Use heap structure for in-place comparison sorting and repeated extreme selection.
outcomes: Implement or trace heapsort and compare it with merge sort and quicksort.

modules.title: Graph representation and traversal
modules.description: Model relationships and explore reachable structure systematically.

skills.title: Graph terminology and models
skills.description: Distinguish directed, undirected, weighted, cyclic, connected and simple graph properties.
outcomes: Model a real relationship problem as vertices and edges with justified graph properties.

skills.title: Adjacency list and matrix
skills.description: Choose representation by graph density, required operations and memory cost.
outcomes: Implement and compare list and matrix representations, including a two-dimensional matrix.

skills.title: Breadth-first and depth-first search
skills.description: Maintain visited state and a queue or stack to traverse without infinite repetition.
outcomes: Implement, trace and test BFS and DFS on disconnected and cyclic graphs.

skills.title: Components and cycle detection
skills.description: Apply traversal state to connected components and directed or undirected cycle detection.
outcomes: Implement and test component counting and one appropriate cycle detector.

modules.title: Directed and weighted graph algorithms
modules.description: Order dependencies and optimize path or connection costs under stated assumptions.

skills.title: Topological ordering
skills.description: Order a directed acyclic graph and detect when dependencies contain a cycle.
outcomes: Implement and test Kahn or DFS topological sorting on valid and cyclic inputs.

skills.title: Unweighted and Dijkstra shortest paths
skills.description: Use BFS for unit edges and Dijkstra for nonnegative weights with priority updates and predecessor reconstruction.
outcomes: Select, implement and test a shortest-path algorithm while stating invalid weight assumptions.

skills.title: Minimum spanning trees
skills.description: Use Prim or Kruskal to connect an undirected weighted graph with minimum total edge cost.
outcomes: Implement and test one MST algorithm and distinguish MST from shortest-path trees.

skills.title: All-pairs path reasoning
skills.description: Use Floyd-Warshall dynamic programming for small dense graphs and interpret unreachable or negative-cycle cases.
outcomes: Implement and trace all-pairs shortest paths over an adjacency matrix.

modules.title: Disjoint sets
modules.description: Maintain dynamic partitions for connectivity and Kruskal-style algorithms.

skills.title: Make, find and union
skills.description: Represent disjoint components and query or combine their representatives.
outcomes: Implement and test a basic parent-array disjoint-set structure.

skills.title: Union by rank or size
skills.description: Attach smaller or shallower trees to control height.
outcomes: Implement and trace union by rank or size while preserving representatives.

skills.title: Path compression
skills.description: Shorten find paths while preserving component identity and explain its amortized benefit conceptually.
outcomes: Implement path compression and compare operation traces before and after optimization.

skills.title: Connectivity applications
skills.description: Use disjoint sets for incremental connectivity, cycle checks and spanning-tree construction.
outcomes: Solve and test a connectivity problem using the disjoint-set API.

modules.title: Greedy, backtracking and dynamic programming
modules.description: Recognize and justify major algorithm-design strategies rather than memorizing solutions.

skills.title: Greedy choice and counterexamples
skills.description: Make locally optimal choices only when exchange or structural reasoning supports global correctness.
outcomes: Design a greedy solution, state its ordering and produce a counterexample when the property fails.

skills.title: Backtracking search
skills.description: Choose, recurse, undo and prune a decision tree while preserving candidate state.
outcomes: Implement and test a permutation, subset or constraint-placement solver with pruning.

skills.title: Overlapping subproblems and memoization
skills.description: Identify repeated state, define a recurrence and cache results with a complete state key.
outcomes: Convert an exponential recursive solution into a tested memoized solution.

skills.title: Tabulation and state reconstruction
skills.description: Order subproblems bottom-up, optimize storage when justified and reconstruct a chosen solution.
outcomes: Design and implement one- and two-dimensional dynamic programs with explanation of state and transition.

modules.title: Sequence and range problem patterns
modules.description: Apply reusable reasoning patterns while still deriving correctness and complexity.

skills.title: Two-pointer and partition scans
skills.description: Move coordinated indexes under a monotonic or partition invariant.
outcomes: Recognize, implement and justify a two-pointer sequence solution.

skills.title: Sliding windows
skills.description: Maintain an incremental fixed or variable range summary instead of recomputing each interval.
outcomes: Implement and analyze fixed and variable sliding-window problems.

skills.title: Monotonic stack and queue
skills.description: Discard dominated candidates while preserving ordered potential answers.
outcomes: Implement and explain a next-greater or range-extreme problem with a monotonic structure.

skills.title: Intervals and sweep ordering
skills.description: Sort interval events to merge, select or count overlaps under explicit boundary semantics.
outcomes: Implement and test interval merging or scheduling with touching-boundary cases.

modules.title: Cross-language implementation and mastery
modules.description: Separate conceptual skill from implementation details and demonstrate robust transfer.

skills.title: Language representation map
skills.description: Map conceptual arrays, nodes, maps, sets, queues, heaps and graphs to idiomatic selected-language facilities.
outcomes: Explain representation and ownership differences for one structure across two supported languages.

skills.title: Language-switch diagnostic
skills.description: Retest implementation prerequisites after a switch while preserving independent conceptual evidence.
outcomes: Complete equivalent implementation, test and debug tasks or receive a targeted remediation path.

skills.title: Adversarial and property tests
skills.description: Test empty, singleton, duplicate, sorted, reverse, disconnected, cyclic, malformed and size-boundary inputs as applicable.
outcomes: Create a reusable test matrix that distinguishes algorithm correctness from a few passing examples.

skills.title: Comparative DSA capstone
skills.description: Solve an integrated problem with multiple candidate structures or strategies and defend the final choice.
outcomes: Deliver, test and explain a selected-language solution with correctness argument, complexity analysis and alternative comparison.

exit_outcomes: Specify, implement and test core linear, associative, hierarchical and graph data structures in the selected language.
Derive time and auxiliary-space complexity and use invariants or counterexamples to reason about correctness.
Select and justify search, sort, traversal, greedy, backtracking, divide-and-conquer or dynamic-programming strategies.
Solve array, string, two-dimensional grid, list, tree, heap and graph problems using appropriate reusable patterns.
Switch supported implementation language without losing conceptual mastery and remediate only failed language-specific prerequisites.
